# spotify-web-api

You can use Spotify's Web API to discover music and podcasts, manage your Spotify library, control audio playback, and much more. Browse our available Web API endpoints using the sidebar at left, or via the navigation bar on top of this page on smaller screens.

In order to make successful Web API requests your app will need a valid access token. One can be obtained through <a href="https://developer.spotify.com/documentation/general/guides/authorization-guide/">OAuth 2.0</a>.

The base URI for all Web API requests is `https://api.spotify.com/v1`.

Need help? See our <a href="https://developer.spotify.com/documentation/web-api/guides/">Web API guides</a> for more information, or visit the <a href="https://community.spotify.com/t5/Spotify-for-Developers/bd-p/Spotify_Developer">Spotify for Developers community forum</a> to ask questions and connect with other developers.


This file is the agent-facing overview of the `spotify-web-api` CLI. It explains what the tool does and how to use it well — *not* every flag of every command. For per-command details run `spotify-web-api <command> --help` or `spotify-web-api <command> --schema` (returns JSON).

## Install

The binary is `spotify-web-api`. Build from source or download a release.

## Authentication

- OAuth2 — run `spotify-web-api login` to authorize, `spotify-web-api logout` to revoke

Run `spotify-web-api configure` to set credentials interactively.

## Commands

spotify-web-api groups operations by resource. One or two examples per group below — for the full set of commands and flags use `spotify-web-api <group> --help`.

### `albums`

```bash
spotify-web-api albums artists-get-albums-by-id --id <string>    # Get Artist's Albums

```

```bash
spotify-web-api albums check-saved --ids <string>    # Check User's Saved Albums

```

List all commands in this group: `spotify-web-api albums --help`

### `artists`

```bash
spotify-web-api artists artists-get-catalog-info --id <string>    # Get Artist

```

```bash
spotify-web-api artists get-albums-by-id --id <string>    # Get Artist's Albums

```

List all commands in this group: `spotify-web-api artists --help`

### `audiobooks`

```bash
spotify-web-api audiobooks check-user-saved --ids <string>    # Check User's Saved Audiobooks

```

```bash
spotify-web-api audiobooks get-catalog-info --id <string>    # Get an Audiobook

```

List all commands in this group: `spotify-web-api audiobooks --help`

### `categories`

```bash
spotify-web-api categories get-single --category-id <string>    # Get Single Browse Category

```

```bash
spotify-web-api categories list-several    # Get Several Browse Categories

```

List all commands in this group: `spotify-web-api categories --help`

### `chapters`

```bash
spotify-web-api chapters audiobooks-get-chapters-by-id --id <string>    # Get Audiobook Chapters

```

```bash
spotify-web-api chapters get-chapter-info --id <string>    # Get a Chapter

```

List all commands in this group: `spotify-web-api chapters --help`

### `episodes`

```bash
spotify-web-api episodes check-saved-episodes --ids <string>    # Check User's Saved Episodes

```

```bash
spotify-web-api episodes get-several --ids <string>    # Get Several Episodes

```

List all commands in this group: `spotify-web-api episodes --help`

### `genres`

```bash
spotify-web-api genres get-available-seeds    # Get Available Genre Seeds

```

List all commands in this group: `spotify-web-api genres --help`

### `library`

```bash
spotify-web-api library albums-check-saved --ids <string>    # Check User's Saved Albums

```

```bash
spotify-web-api library albums-get-saved    # Get User's Saved Albums

```

List all commands in this group: `spotify-web-api library --help`

### `markets`

```bash
spotify-web-api markets list-available    # Get Available Markets

```

List all commands in this group: `spotify-web-api markets --help`

### `player`

```bash
spotify-web-api player add-item-to-queue --uri <string>    # Add Item to Playback Queue

```

```bash
spotify-web-api player get-available-devices    # Get Available Devices

```

List all commands in this group: `spotify-web-api player --help`

### `playlists`

```bash
spotify-web-api playlists add-items --playlist-id <string>    # Add Items to Playlist

```

```bash
spotify-web-api playlists create-playlist --user-id <string>    # Create Playlist

```

List all commands in this group: `spotify-web-api playlists --help`

### `search`

```bash
spotify-web-api search spotify-catalog-info --q <string> --type <array>    # Search for Item

```

List all commands in this group: `spotify-web-api search --help`

### `shows`

```bash
spotify-web-api shows check-saved-shows --ids <string>    # Check User's Saved Shows

```

```bash
spotify-web-api shows get-episodes-by-id --id <string>    # Get Show Episodes

```

List all commands in this group: `spotify-web-api shows --help`

### `tracks`

```bash
spotify-web-api tracks albums-get-tracks-by-id --id <string>    # Get Album Tracks

```

```bash
spotify-web-api tracks artists-get-top --id <string>    # Get Artist's Top Tracks

```

List all commands in this group: `spotify-web-api tracks --help`

### `users`

```bash
spotify-web-api users check-following-artists-users --type <string> --ids <string>    # Check If User Follows Artists or Users

```

```bash
spotify-web-api users check-if-follows-playlist --playlist-id <string> --ids <string>    # Check if Users Follow Playlist

```

List all commands in this group: `spotify-web-api users --help`


## Output and parsing

All output is JSON by default and goes to stdout. Errors go to stderr as a single-line JSON object — stdout stays clean for piping.

```bash
spotify-web-api <cmd> --jq <path>          # extract fields without jq installed (GJSON syntax)
spotify-web-api <cmd> -o yaml              # change format: json (default), yaml, table, compact, raw, pretty
spotify-web-api <cmd> --dry-run            # print the HTTP request without sending it
spotify-web-api <cmd> --schema             # JSON schema of inputs and outputs for that command
```

GJSON path examples:
- `--jq id` — scalar
- `--jq items.#.id` — every id from an array
- `--jq "items.#(active==true)#"` — filter array by condition

## Exit codes

Branch on `$?` rather than parsing stderr:

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| 0 | success |
| 1 | unknown error |
| 2 | auth failed (401 / 403) |
| 3 | not found (404) |
| 4 | validation error (400 / 422) |
| 5 | rate limited (429) |
| 6 | server error (5xx) |
| 7 | network error |

Error JSON shape on stderr:
```json
{"error":true,"code":"not_found","status":404,"message":"...","exit_code":3}
```

## Common workflows

### List then fetch one
```bash
ID=$(spotify-web-api <group> list --jq "items.0.id")
spotify-web-api <group> get --id "$ID"
```

### Capture a created ID
```bash
ID=$(spotify-web-api <group> create [flags] --jq id)
```

### Safe destructive call
```bash
spotify-web-api <group> delete --id X --dry-run    # inspect first
spotify-web-api <group> delete --id X              # exit 0 = deleted, 3 = not found
```

### Branch on exit code
```bash
spotify-web-api <cmd> [flags]
case $? in
  0) : success ;;
  2) echo "fix credentials" ;;
  3) echo "not found, skip" ;;
  5) sleep 10 ; retry ;;
esac
```

## Discovering more

```bash
spotify-web-api --help                              # top-level groups
spotify-web-api <group> --help                      # commands in a group
spotify-web-api <group> <cmd> --help                # flags + description
spotify-web-api <group> <cmd> --schema              # JSON schema
spotify-web-api agent-instructions                  # this file, embedded in the binary
```
